0: Impossible. Not even Geass could alter this reality.
0%. Absolute zero. Like Suzaku's chances at a healthy relationship.
This probability equals the chance that I would refuse pizza. Which is zero.
1: One percent is not a chance. It's a statistical rounding error.
Practically impossible. But I've seen stranger things over 600 years.
2: Two percent. That isn't even hope — that's self-delusion.
The odds are so slim that even Lelouch wouldn't bet on this.
3: Three percent. About as likely as finding good pizza in Antarctica.
4: Four percent. Only a miracle would help. And I don't believe in miracles.
Odds at critical failure level in D&D. Roll a natural one.
5: Five percent. Like the chance of pulling an SSR without a pity.
One in twenty. Technically possible, but I wouldn't count on it.
6: Six percent. Slightly better than nothing. Slightly.
Faint, but not hopeless. Like Mao's attempts to bring me back.
7: Seven percent. Lucky number, but the odds are still sad.
You could try, but I wouldn't bet my pizza on it.
8: Eight percent. People have won the lottery with worse.
Unlikely. But sometimes the unlikely happens — I'm living proof.
9: Nine percent. Almost ten, but not ten. As always, just barely falling short.
There's a chance, but it's like Wi-Fi in a basement — barely detectable.
10: Ten percent. Every tenth time — that's no longer coincidence, that's a pattern.
One in ten. Lelouch has won with worse odds.
Ten percent is like finding parking downtown. Theoretically possible.
11: Eleven percent. Slightly more than one would want for 'impossible'.
An odd chance. Fate likes odd numbers.
12: Twelve percent. Like the chance rain won't ruin your picnic. In London.
About one in eight. Things have been worse.
13: Thirteen percent. An unlucky number for an unlucky outcome.
A devil's dozen percent. The superstitious are already panicking.
14: Fourteen percent. Don't bet your dinner on this.
The probability is small, but enough to make you nervous.
15: Fifteen percent. Once every sixth or seventh time. Not entirely negligible.
One and a half chances in ten. For an adventurer, that's an invitation.
Fifteen percent — enough to hope, not enough to count on.
16: Sixteen percent. Like rolling a six on a die.
Not the worst outcome. I've seen worse.
17: Seventeen percent. Roughly one in six. Will you risk it?
On the edge. Like balancing between hope and reality.
18: Eighteen percent. The coming of age of probability. No longer a child, but not yet an adult.
Almost every fifth time. That's something.
19: Nineteen percent. On the threshold of twenty, but didn't quite reach it.
Close to something meaningful, but not yet.
20: Twenty percent. Every fifth time. That's noticeable.
One in five. About like the chance Lelouch tells the truth.
Twenty percent is one fifth of certainty.
21: Twenty-one. Blackjack of probability. Might get lucky.
Slightly more than one in five. Decent for a lottery, bad for a plan.
22: Twenty-two percent. Double twos. Symmetrical, but not much.
Every fourth or fifth time. Depends on luck.
23: Twenty-three percent. The conspiracy theory number. Coincidence? I don't think so.
Almost a quarter. No longer trivial.
24: Twenty-four percent. Like hours in a day — seems like a lot, but always feels short.
The chances are growing. Slowly, but they're growing.
25: Twenty-five percent. Exactly a quarter. One in four.
A quarter of probability. Like the chance of guessing a die face on the first try.
Every fourth time — that's statistics, not luck.
26: Twenty-six percent. Slightly more than a quarter. The cards are falling nicely.
More than a quarter. Worth considering.
27: Twenty-seven percent. Three cubed. Mathematically beautiful, practically average.
Not as bad as it seems. But not as good as one might wish.
28: Twenty-eight percent. Almost a third. Almost.
Closing in on something. Like a ship approaching shore through fog.
29: Twenty-nine percent. On the threshold of thirty. Something is about to change.
So close to a third that you could round up. But I don't round.
30: Thirty percent. Almost a third. Already worth considering as an option.
Three in ten. Bad for a battle. Excellent for a miracle.
Thirty percent — that's when you should have a backup plan.
31: Thirty-one percent. Slightly more than a third of the way to confidence.
Just over thirty. Like the age after which things get more interesting.
32: Thirty-two percent. A power of two. Computers approve.
About every third time. If lucky.
33: Thirty-three percent. Exactly a third. Classic 'maybe'.
One in three. Odds where Russian roulette would get boring.
A third of probability. Enough to risk. Not enough to be certain.
34: Thirty-four percent. Slightly more than a third. The scales begin to tip.
There's a chance, and it's not all that small.
35: Thirty-five percent. Every third time plus a little. Intriguing.
More than a third. Already a serious chance for those who know how to wait.
36: Thirty-six percent. Like body temperature — normal, but not hot.
37: The optimal stopping point by secretary theory. Coincidence?
38: Thirty-eight percent. Closing in on forty. The situation is improving.
No longer a small probability. Can no longer be ignored.
39: Thirty-nine percent. Almost forty. So close and so far at the same time.
On the threshold of moving to a different league of probability.
40: Forty percent. Two in five. This is serious now.
Forty percent — like a coin that lands tails more often, but heads is possible.
Almost half. Enough to factor into calculations.
41: Forty-one percent. Slightly less than half. Like a crescent moon.
Close to even odds. Very close.
42: Forty-two percent. The answer to the ultimate question of the universe. Coincidence? Unlikely.
Forty-two — the number that contains the meaning of everything. Including this forecast.
43: Forty-three percent. Closer to half than to a third.
The odds are nearly equal. Nearly.
44: Forty-four percent. Double fours. Steadily near half.
Practically a coin flip. But the coin is slightly bent.
45: Forty-five percent. Almost half. Just a little short.
Forty-five — when it feels like the odds are even, but they aren't.
Close to fifty-fifty. Fate is wavering.
46: Forty-six percent. The scales are almost balanced.
Just under half. As always, missing by just a little.
47: Three percent short of half. So close.
48: Forty-eight percent. Practically a coin flip. But life isn't a coin.
Nearly equal odds. Everything depends on small details.
49: Forty-nine percent. One percent from half. Dramatic.
So close to fifty that the difference is just luck.
50: Fifty-fifty. Flip a coin. Or ask me again.
Exactly half. Perfect balance between 'yes' and 'no'.
Fifty percent. Like Schrödinger — it will both happen and not happen simultaneously.
The coin is in the air. Where it lands is not up to me.
51: Fifty-one percent. Just over half. The scales tipped.
A minimal majority. Like winning an election by a single vote.
52: Fifty-two percent. Balance broken in favor of 'yes'.
More than half. A trend is forming.
53: Fifty-three percent. The odds are on your side. Slightly.
Just more than a coin flip. Fate is being gracious.
54: Fifty-four percent. Probability is rising. Don't rush, but be ready.
Confidence is quietly winning over doubt.
55: Just over half. The wind is blowing in the right direction.
56: Fifty-six percent. Statistics are on your side.
More than half — already a decent bet.
57: Fifty-seven percent. Good odds, but not perfect.
Moderately likely. Like rain under a cloudy sky.
58: Fifty-eight percent. The needle is confidently drifting right.
More yes than no. But I would still take precautions.
59: Fifty-nine percent. Almost sixty. The trend is clear.
The odds are noticeably above average. That's encouraging.
60: Sixty percent. Three in five. This will likely happen.
Sixty percent — that's no longer luck, that's probability.
Three in five. I'd bet a slice of pizza on it.
61: Good odds. Not perfect, but good.
62: Sixty-two percent. A perfectly respectable probability.
More than three in five. Optimistic.
63: Sixty-three percent. Almost two-thirds. Serious odds.
Fate is leaning in your favor.
64: Sixty-four percent. Two to the sixth power. Bit by bit toward certainty.
Good odds. I'd take the risk.
65: Sixty-five percent. Two-thirds of the way to 'definitely'.
The odds are on your side. Firmly on your side.
Sixty-five — when you can start making plans.
66: Sixty-six percent. The number of the beast minus six hundred. Almost two-thirds.
Two-thirds probability. That's already a serious claim.
67: Sixty-seven percent. Two-thirds. Luck is on your side.
68: Sixty-eight percent. One standard deviation. Statisticians applaud.
More likely yes. Even I would agree.
69: Sixty-nine percent. Nice. The odds are too.
A pleasant number with a pleasant probability.
70: Seventy percent. Seven in ten. Quite likely.
Seventy — when you can stop doubting. Almost.
Seven in ten. Lelouch built plans with less certainty than this.
71: Seventy-one percent. Confidence grows with every percent.
73: Seventy-three percent. The best prime number and excellent odds.
High probability. You can count on it.
74: Seventy-four percent. Three-quarters almost in hand.
Very likely. Fate is clearly on your side.
75: Three in four. If this were roulette, the bet is nearly a sure thing.
Seventy-five — when even I start to believe.
76: Seventy-six percent. More than three-quarters. Impressive.
77: Seventy-seven percent. Double sevens. Luck favors you.
A lucky number with high probability. The stars have aligned.
78: Almost four in five. That's nearly a guarantee.
79: Seventy-nine percent. On the threshold of eighty. Serious.
The odds are so high that doubt would be foolish.
80: Eighty percent. Four in five. Most likely — yes.
Eighty percent — that's no longer probability, that's practically a fact.
Four in five. I'd bet all my pizza on it.
81: Very high probability. Prepare for it to happen.
82: Doubts melt like ice cream in the sun.
83: Eighty-three percent. Five in six. Serious confidence.
Extremely high probability. I would already be taking action.
84: Eighty-four percent. This is almost predetermined.
So high that 'no' would be a surprise.
85: Eighty-five percent. Almost nine in ten. Impressive.
The odds are so good that even I'm surprised.
Eighty-five — when the plan can almost not fail.
86: Eighty-six percent. Probability is on your side unconditionally.
Six in seven. That's no longer a chance, that's a trend.
87: Eighty-seven percent. Fate is practically decided.
So likely that the opposite would be a miracle.
88: Eighty-eight percent. Double happiness in Asian culture. And in probability too.
Almost nine in ten. That's near inevitability.
89: Eighty-nine percent. A prime number and a simple truth — this will happen.
On the threshold of ninety. Practically settled.
90: Ninety percent. Nine in ten. This will happen.
Ninety percent — when even pessimists agree.
Nine in ten. I would sign a contract on these terms.
91: The odds against are so small they're not worth thinking about.
92: Doubting this is like doubting the sunrise.
93: Ninety-three percent. This is no longer a probability, it's a prediction.
So high that the opposite would be an anomaly.
94: Ninety-four percent. Practically one hundred percent certainty.
Likely enough that you can plan your next steps.
95: Ninety-five percent. Statistical significance level. Science approves.
Ninety-five — when even scientists call it 'conclusive'.
Nineteen in twenty. This is practically a law of nature.
96: Ninety-six percent. Refusal is almost impossible.
The probability is so high there is nothing left to discuss.
97: Ninety-seven percent. Three percent for a miracle. And miracles are rare.
This will happen. I'm almost certain.
98: Ninety-eight percent. Two percent for the impossible.
If this doesn't happen, I'll eat pizza with pineapple. Which means it will happen.
99: Ninety-nine percent. One percent from absolute. Almost destiny.
So likely I'm prepared to swear on my Code of Immortality.
100: One hundred percent. Absolute certainty. This will happen. Period.
Inevitable. Like my immortality and my love of pizza.
One hundred percent probability. Not even Geass is needed to see that.
